meaning: n.纠正,整顿,校正,精馏,整流
examples: To be responsible for the rectification and closure of coal mines.
To review and verify the reports on the rectification of related financial institutions.
Criteria, time-limits and measures for quality rectification of the imported technology and liabilities for risks;
Rights of access, rectification and erasure all exist under our current rules, the 1995 EU Data Protection Directive.
Firstly, 250, 000 borrowers were left out of the scope of the rectification programme because their mortgage details were not recorded properly.
